summary: A broad production run of the Reverser pe-constructor-installs analyzer over the bootstrap/config setup range 0x140021A40..0x140023E52 recovered a dense cluster of client-owned constructor installs. The scan found 13 candidate install patterns from 42 allocator calls, including the previously mapped auth controller at client+0x198D0 and additional bootstrap-owned slots spanning client+0x19858 through client+0x199B8.
confidence: high for the direct allocator -> constructor -> slot-store rows that install into RDI-relative client slots; medium for the duplicated client+0x199B0 candidates because they share the same constructor/store endpoint; low for the R14+0x10 direct-slot row because it appears to be an internal nested helper/store pattern rather than a top-level client-slot constructor install.

client_slot_install_candidates.note: Shares the same constructor call and store endpoint as the following client+0x199B0 row; treat as overlapping native evidence until the analyzer grows de-duplication.
Likely the concrete allocation backing the client+0x199B0 install; verify with constructor use-sites/destructor ownership before assigning semantics.
Allocation-size recovery did not resolve in this row, but the constructor and slot-pointer store pattern are direct.

client_slot_install_candidates.semantic_role: login/auth controller pointer, matching the prior auth-controller constructor-chain milestone

non_client_or_low_priority_candidates.note: This does not install into the client owner register RDI. Treat it as nested helper/internal-store noise unless later call-chain evidence proves a real object lifetime.

adjacent_slot_negative_evidence.result: No constructor-install pattern matched when filtering the broad bootstrap scan to slot offset 0x19838.
adjacent_slot_negative_evidence.interpretation: Preserves the prior manual conclusion that client+0x19838 is initialized by a different inline descriptor/replacement pattern around 0x14002233B..0x1400223C4, including a 0x60 allocation and descriptor 0x140B5DB18, not by the constructor-return install pattern recognized here.

adjacent_slot_negative_evidence.result: No constructor-install pattern matched when filtering the broad bootstrap scan to slot offset 0x19830.
adjacent_slot_negative_evidence.interpretation: client+0x19830 remains an adjacent replacement/cleanup slot candidate rather than a native allocator -> constructor -> returned-pointer install in this pass.

relationship_to_prior_findings: Confirms the previously mapped auth-controller allocation, constructor call, and client+0x198D0 install without changing its semantics.

Expands the bootstrap/config setup range from a single auth-controller milestone into an object-lifetime worklist rooted at client+0x19858, client+0x19870, client+0x19878, client+0x198C0, client+0x198D0, client+0x198E0, client+0x198E8, client+0x19900, client+0x19940, client+0x199B0, and client+0x199B8.
Preserves the huffman/archive and config-key cluster conclusions: the same 0x140021A40 range is a dense bootstrap initializer, not only an archive-key setup function.
Separates constructor-return installs from nearby inline descriptor/replacement slots, which keeps client+0x19838 and client+0x19830 from being overtyped.
opennxt_947_server_implications: OpenNXT 947 compatibility work should treat the auth/login path as one object in a larger bootstrap owner lattice. Failures around login keys, JS5 setup, archive/huffman setup, or session roots may share construction order and owner lifetime constraints in this client range.

The client appears to construct several long-lived client-owned service/controller objects before or during archive/config setup. Server-side parity tests should capture startup order, not only isolated packet builders.

client+0x198D0 remains the strongest auth-controller anchor, while the surrounding slots give concrete future targets for mapping JS5, network/bootstrap, scheduler, cache, and login-session consumers.

reverser_tool_feedback.change_this_milestone: No code change was needed; this milestone is the first broad production use of the newly added pe-constructor-installs analyzer against the 947 bootstrap range.
followup_tool_improvements: Add de-duplication for overlapping allocator candidates that lead to the same constructor call and slot store, as seen at client+0x199B0.
Add optional owner-register filters or suppressions so non-client nested stores such as R14+0x10 can be separated from RDI-owned client installs.
Add a summary exporter that groups constructor installs by slot offset, constructor VA, allocation size, and confidence so future heartbeat passes produce concise map artifacts automatically.

next_targets: Run slot use-site and destructor scans for each client+0x19858..0x199B8 candidate to attach semantic names.
Enrich each constructor with literals, direct calls, and descriptor/vtable-like writes to sort auth, JS5, cache/archive, network/bootstrap, and scheduler roles.
Fold the observed de-duplication and owner-register filtering needs back into pe-constructor-installs once another broad scan confirms the same noise patterns.
Continue from the strongest unmapped slots near the auth controller first: client+0x198C0, client+0x198E0, client+0x198E8, and client+0x19900.
